title: Genuine Parts Said to Receive O’Reilly Interest in Auto Unit
summary: Genuine Parts Co. has attracted a cash bid for its auto-parts arm from O’Reilly Automotive Inc. as it seeks to refocus on its industrials business, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Fed and ECB Seen Diverging in French Snapshot of War Aftermath
summary: The Federal Reserve will still need to hike interest rates this year despite Thursday’s numbers pointing to labor-market weakness, according to two French chief economists.

title: Buddhism: China's New Soft Power Front
summary: China is investing billions to shape the future of Buddhism. From temples and universities to the battle over the Dalai Lama's successor, Beijing is using faith as a tool of influence and fueling a growing soft power rivalry with India. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: European Stocks Head for Best Week Since May: Markets Wrap
summary: Stocks rose as the latest round of jitters about the artificial-intelligence trade subsided, with Europe’s benchmark rising to an all-time high. The dollar touched a two-week low while gold extended gains.

title: The AI Trade Is Losing One of Its Key Signals
summary: At a time when markets are growing uneasy over whether the enormous sums being poured into artificial intelligence will ever pay off, the prices the sector commands for each unit of usage are drifting lower.

title: Oil Wavers as Persian Gulf Flows Surge Toward Pre-War Levels
summary: Oil prices wavered in thin trading as the recovery of Persian Gulf flows fomented a new surplus in key parts of the global market.

title: Gold Heads for First Weekly Gain Since May on Easing Fed Outlook
summary: Gold headed for its first weekly gain since May as traders dialed down their expectations of Federal Reserve rate hikes.

title: Copper Tracks Dollar Swings as Traders Weigh US Rate Outlook
summary: Copper edged higher, tracking small swings in the US dollar as investors continue to assess the outlook for US monetary policy.

title: Carlyle Hires Banks for $400 Million India IPO of RCM Business
summary: Carlyle Group Inc. has hired JM Financial Ltd. and Goldman Sachs Group Inc. to advise on a potential initial public offering of its India-based healthcare revenue cycle management business, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: iCON Infrastructure to Buy Northern Fiber in Rescue Deal
summary: Lenders of Northern Fiber Holding GmbH, a struggling German fiber-network operator formerly owned by UBS Asset Management, are finalizing the sale of the company to iCON Infrastructure to avoid a looming insolvency.

title: Pubs to Get Short-Term World Cup Boost
summary: UK pubs will be allowed to stay open for Monday’s early morning match, but the overall benefit of the World Cup will likely be short-lived. Bloomberg's Sam Unsed discusses on "The Opening Trade." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Russia Hikes June Subsidies to Refiners as Fuel Shortages Bite
summary: Russia’s subsidy payouts to oil refiners that supply domestic markets jumped more than six-fold in June from a year earlier, as Moscow sought to avoid shortages by keeping more fuel at home.

title: EU Agrees to Cut ESG Reporting Requirements for Asset Managers
summary: Money managers in Europe won’t have to report ESG data on all the assets they hold, under revised disclosure requirements.

title: What Natural Gas’s Ascendancy Says About the US
summary: Fracking, booming power demand and enthusiastic political support have underpinned the fuel’s rise, but for how long?

title: Adani Group Secures $15 Billion Capital This Week as US Legal Clouds Clear
summary: In one week, billionaire Gautam Adani has announced nearly $15 billion in investment commitments across its ports, mining and flagship businesses.

title: CVC Plans €1.2 Billion of High-Yield Bonds to Fund Irca Buyout
summary: CVC Capital Partners Plc is set to finance its buyout of Italian food-ingredients maker Irca SpA with €1.2 billion ($1.4 billion) of high-yield bonds, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Mamoon Hamid on the Importance of Identifying Potential
summary: Barry sits down with Kleiner Perkins Partner, Mamoon Hamid. They discuss Mamoon's thoughts on the AI revolution and his approach to early AI investing.  Mamoon also breaks down how he became an early investor in giants like Slack, Figma, and how Kleiner Perkins assesses the investments they missed.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: China’s State Iron Ore Buyer Extends Restrictions on Fortescue
summary: China’s state-backed iron ore buyer has expanded its curbs on Fortescue Ltd.’s iron ore to include new purchases of its Super Special Fines product and not just portside inventories, escalating its dispute with the Australian miner.

title: Indonesia’s Danantara Wealth Fund Sees Senior Management Shakeup
summary: Indonesian sovereign wealth fund Danantara is reshuffling its management team with a series of new hires, according to people familiar with the matter, as it expands its role under President Prabowo Subianto’s government.

title: Trump Doubles Down on Fed Changes, Burnham Risks Starmer Mistakes  | The Opening Trade 7/2/2026
summary:
President Donald Trump and his allies are renewing a push to reshape the Federal Reserve after the Supreme Court this week blocked an effort to fire Governor Lisa Cook.

Some in the Labour Party are worried Andy Burnham risks repeating the mistake of not being sufficiently prepared for government, as he delays key personnel decisions. Burnham won't announce key appointments until after the selection process for the Labour Party's new leader formally concludes, which could give new ministers a slim window to prepare.

The Opening Trade has everything you need to know as markets open across Europe. With analysis you won't find anywhere else, we break down the biggest stories of the day and speak to top guests who have skin in the game. Hosted by Anna Edwards and Tom Mackenzie. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Investors Warm to Burnham But Say Long-End Gilts Are ‘Unloved’
summary: UK bond investors are gaining confidence in Andy Burnham’s promises of discipline when it comes to borrowing and spending, but not enough to bet on Britain’s long-dated debt.

title: ECB Is 'Comfortable' With Its Base Scenario, Moulin Says
summary: European Central Bank Governing Council member Emmanuel Moulin comments on the outlook for inflation and monetary policy. He talks with Bloomberg's Francine Lacqua at the Aix-en-Provence Economic Forum in France. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Gulf Carriers Eye Boeing, Airbus Jets as Air India Weighs Delays
summary: Airlines in the Persian Gulf are looking to snap up earlier delivery slots for Boeing Co. and Airbus SE aircraft that may be freed up as Air India Ltd. discusses potentially deferring some orders, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Investors Track Tokens for Clues on AI Trade’s Next Move
summary: Investors are tracking AI token prices as they try to figure out where the volatile trade goes next.

title: ECB Is in ‘Good Position’ After June Rate Hike, Moulin Says
summary: The European Central Bank is in a comfortable situation after last month’s interest-rate increase as the falling oil price eases price pressures in the euro zone, Governing Council member Emmanuel Moulin said.

title: Trump Allies Push to Reshape Fed | The Pulse 7/3/2026
summary:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests: Kokou Agbo-Bloua, SocGen CIB Global Head of Economics, Cross-Asset & Quant Research; Philippe Aghion, Nobel Prize in Economics Winner; Estelle Brachlianoff, Veolia CEO (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Stocks Post Largest Outflows in Over Three Months, BofA Says
summary: Investors are turning away from US stocks at the fastest pace since March, according to Bank of America Corp.

title: HSBC, Standard Chartered Weigh SRTs as Asia-Linked Deals Ramp Up
summary: HSBC Holdings Plc is laying the groundwork for a significant risk transfer linked to a portfolio of Asia-Pacific loans as it ramps up use of the hedging instrument.

title: Taiwanese Brokerage Yuanta Taps Banks for $1.3 Billion of Loans
summary: Yuanta Securities Co., Taiwan’s largest brokerage, and its subsidiary, are seeking up to NT$42 billion ($1.3 billion) through two syndicated loans, according to people familiar with the matter, a fundraising that highlights surging demand for financial services amid the stock boom.
